skill.name: wordpress-plugin-development
skill.description: WordPress plugin development workflow covering plugin architecture, hooks, admin interfaces, REST API, and security best practices.
skill.summary: Meta-skill workflow bundle for creating WordPress plugins with proper architecture and security.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer produced false positives by misinterpreting Markdown backtick code references as shell commands. The SKILL.md file is a documentation-only workflow bundle that references other skills for WordPress plugin development. No executable code, no command injection, no cryptographic operations, and no malicious patterns detected.

content.user_title: Build WordPress plugins with AI assistance
content.value_statement: Creating WordPress plugins requires understanding complex architecture and security patterns. This skill guides you through a structured workflow for building production-ready plugins.
actual_capabilities: Guide through complete WordPress plugin scaffolding and directory structure
Explain WordPress hooks system including actions and filters
Help create admin interfaces with settings pages and menus
Design custom database tables and CRUD operations for plugins
Build WordPress REST API endpoints with proper permissions
Implement WordPress security practices including nonces and capability checks
limitations: References external skills that may not be available in your environment
Does not execute code directly - provides guidance only
WordPress-specific patterns may not apply to non-WordPress PHP projects
Requires existing WordPress installation for testing plugin code

use_cases.title: WordPress Developer
use_cases.description: Create custom plugins to extend WordPress functionality for client projects

use_cases.title: Agency Development Team
use_cases.description: Standardize plugin development workflow across team members for consistent quality

use_cases.title: WordPress Product Builder
use_cases.description: Develop commercial plugins for distribution on WordPress.org or CodeCanyon

prompt_templates.title: Basic Plugin Scaffolding
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a new WordPress plugin called 'My Custom Plugin'. Set up the main plugin file with proper headers, activation hooks, and directory structure following WordPress coding standards.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new plugin project from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Custom Admin Settings Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a WordPress admin settings page for my plugin with a text field, dropdown select, and checkbox. Include proper options registration, settings sanitization, and admin menu integration.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding configuration options to an existing plugin

prompt_templates.title: REST API Endpoint Development
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a WordPress REST API endpoint for my plugin that accepts POST requests with user data. Include permission callbacks, request validation, and proper error responses.
prompt_templates.scenario: Exposing plugin functionality via REST API

prompt_templates.title: Custom Database Table Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a custom database table for my WordPress plugin to store user activity logs. Create the table on plugin activation with proper indexing and include functions for inserting, querying, and deleting records.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding persistent data storage to a plugin

output_examples.input: Create a WordPress plugin that adds a custom post type for 'Portfolio' projects
output_examples.output: A complete plugin with main file, custom post type registration, admin columns, and meta box support for portfolio items.

output_examples.input: Add a shortcode that displays recent blog posts in a grid layout
output_examples.output: A shortcode handler with configurable attributes for post count, layout style, and custom CSS classes.

best_practices: Always prefix function names, classes, and database tables with your plugin slug to avoid conflicts
Sanitize all user input and escape all output to prevent XSS and SQL injection attacks
Use WordPress hooks system instead of modifying core files or other plugins directly
anti_patterns: Never directly modify WordPress core files - use hooks and filters instead
Avoid storing sensitive data in plain text - use encryption for API keys and passwords
Do not use global variables for plugin state - use class properties or options table

faq.question: Do I need to install any dependencies to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidance and code generation. You need a standard WordPress development environment with PHP and a text editor or IDE.

faq.question: Can this skill create plugins that work with specific WordPress themes?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can help you build theme-agnostic plugins or customize output for specific theme frameworks.

faq.question: Does this skill handle WordPress multisite compatibility?
faq.answer: The skill can guide you on multisite-specific considerations, but you should specify if you need multisite support in your requests.

faq.question: Can I create WooCommerce extensions with this skill?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on core WordPress plugin development. For WooCommerce-specific features, use the woocommerce-woocommerce skill bundle.

faq.question: Does this skill help with plugin submission to WordPress.org?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers WordPress coding standards and security requirements needed for plugin repository approval.

faq.question: What PHP version does this skill target?
faq.answer: The skill generates code compatible with PHP 7.4 or higher, which covers most WordPress installations. Specify if you need older version support.
